twin_relationship: Production-render variant of scene 07 (Coral Structures Tutorial). Core particle→volume architecture identical; this scene adds lighting + RS camera + 2 RS materials + procedural backdrop + region-specific Spherical Fields + a 3rd Nodes Modifier (Normal Masking instead of Mask Y).
purpose: Production-render-ready coral growth scene. Same particle→volume core as scene 07, wrapped with 4-light rig + Redshift Camera + 2 RS materials + procedural Nodes-Mesh backdrop + 2 Spherical Fields for face/shoulder localized coral patches.
visual_result: Head bust framed by RS Camera against a procedural backdrop, with 4-light rig setup. Coral grows in two localized patches (Face + Shoulder regions) gated by the two Spherical Fields. Renders via Redshift.
delta_vs_tutorial: ADDED: 4-light rig (BG Light + Key Light + Rim Light + Bouncecard)
ADDED: Backdrop (180420600 — Nodes Mesh as procedural background plane)
CHANGED: Random Field → 2× Spherical Field (Face + Shoulder) for region-specific gating
ADDED: Plain falloff field as direct child of head (extra layered selection)
CHANGED: Mask Y Nodes Modifier → Normal Masking Nodes Modifier (filters by surface normal direction instead of Y range)
ADDED: Generic Head Bust appears TWICE (once at OM level for rendering reference, once inside Volume Builder for SDF unioning)
RENAMED: Particle Group Grow → Particle Group Meshing (semantic equivalent)
core_architecture_unchanged: Mesh Emitter → Particle Group Init → Field Condition → Kill / Switch Group → Particle Group Meshing → Volume Builder + Subdivision Surface (head + Geometry Axis Nodes Mod) → Volume Mesher → Smoothing → Connect → final mesh. Same R17_particle_to_volume_growth recipe applies.

hosts.om_name: Geometry Axis (×2 instances — head OM-level + Volume-Builder-internal)

hosts.role: post-process: filter output points by surface normal direction

doc_level_objects.name: Plain
doc_level_objects.role: child of head — vertex selection painter

om_classic_generators_unchanged_from_07: Particle Group Init / Particle Group Meshing (1060887 ×2)

key_connections_added_vs_07.from: Spherical Field Face + Spherical Field Shoulder (doc-level)
key_connections_added_vs_07.purpose: Two localized fields combine (probably OR) to produce face+shoulder coral patches — vs Tutorial's single Random Field for distributed coverage.
Additional layered selection painted directly on the head — adds another control for which surface points participate.
Production camera framing for Redshift render.

output_routing: Identical to scene 07 — output is the Connect generator wrapping Volume Mesher (with Smoothing) + Normal Masking Nodes Modifier.
what_is_clever: Twin scene pattern: Tutorial = bare architecture, Render = production-ready wrap of same architecture. Recipe (R17) is shared; render-specific scaffolding (R21+R22+R23+R24) layered on top.

Two Spherical Fields at anatomical positions (Face + Shoulder) replace single Random Field — produces TWO localized coral patches instead of distributed coverage. Demonstrates field combination patterns.

Three Nodes Modifiers stacked at different pipeline stages: Geometry Axis (×2 — preprocesses head twice), Normal Masking (post-processes output by surface normal). Same plugin ID, completely different graph internals.
Generic Head Bust + Geometry Axis stack appears twice — once at OM level, once inside Volume Builder. Twin-instance for cache/perf reasons.
Procedural Nodes-Mesh backdrop demonstrates that even render scaffolding can be Scene Nodes — not just the simulation logic.

gotchas.issue: Same particle-compute timeout as scene 07. f20 stepping caused viewport_screenshot timeout.
gotchas.evidence: Trying f0 + sequential 1..20 → screenshot at f20 timed out.
gotchas.workaround: Per gotcha #59 (play-don't-scrub): trust f0 for production-render visualization (architecture is clear), use playback for animated state.

gotchas.issue: Backdrop is plugin 180420600 (Nodes Mesh simple) — could use either nodespace. Didn't probe in this study.
gotchas.workaround: Probe GetAllNimbusRefs() if studying the backdrop's graph internals.

gotchas.issue: Generic Head Bust appears TWICE in OM tree — could be confused for same instance but probably duplicate copies for separate rendering / volume-input purposes.
gotchas.evidence: Tree walk showed 'Generic Head Bust' under both Subdivision Surface (top-level) and Subdivision Surface (inside Volume Builder).
gotchas.workaround: Treat as separate instances; check if changes in one propagate to the other before assuming linkage.

rebuild_recipe: 1. Apply R17_particle_to_volume_growth recipe from scene 07 (same particle → volume core).
2. Layer the production render setup (parallel to step 1):
a. Add 4-light rig: BG Light + Key Light + Rim Light + Bouncecard plane.
b. Add RS Camera (1057516) and set as active scene camera.
c. Replace single Random Field with 2× Spherical Field (Face + Shoulder) at anatomical positions; wire Field Condition to combine them.
d. Add Plain falloff field as child of head for extra vertex-selection painting.
e. Replace Mask Y Nodes Modifier with Normal Masking Nodes Modifier (180420400).
f. Add Backdrop (180420600 Nodes Mesh) for procedural background plane.
g. Create 2 Redshift materials (BG for backdrop, Corals for coral mesh).
h. Optional: instance Generic Head Bust + Geometry Axis at both OM-level and Volume-Builder-internal positions.

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: production_render_wrap
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Wrap any procedural simulation scene with a production-render layer (lighting + camera + materials + procedural backdrop) without changing the simulation core.
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.node_count: 12 OM objects + 2 materials + 1 Nodes Mesh backdrop
nodes: LIGHTS Null wrapping {BG Light, Key Light, Rim Light, Bouncecard}
Backdrop (180420600 Nodes Mesh) for procedural background
2× material (one for backdrop, one for main mesh)
Optional: localized Spherical Fields for region-specific control
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: Decouples production-render scaffolding from simulation logic. Same R21+R22+R23+R24 recipe wraps ANY simulation scene (R17 coral, R7 RD, R9 infection, R14 ornaments, etc.) for render-ready output without re-authoring the lighting/camera/materials each time.
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: Redshift integration via cinema4d-mcp (for RS Camera + RS Material)
Recipe-composition primitive needed: 'wrap simulation scene S with render layer L'
Multi-Spherical-Field combination pattern in Field Condition needs documentation

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: Twin-scene pattern (tutorial / render) confirms recipes — same architecture proven in two contexts.
Recipe composition is essential: simulation recipe + render recipe = production scene. Don't bake them together.
Procedural Nodes-Mesh backdrops are a lightweight pattern — single host for background plane.
Three Nodes Modifiers in one scene proves stacking is universal: pre-input, mid-pipeline, post-output.
Localized Spherical Fields at anatomical positions = region-specific control. More artist-friendly than single Random Field for character work.

frames: frames/default_f000.png (RS Camera framing, no coral yet — particle sim not advanced)
